content.problemStatement:
You have an array AAA with NNN elements.

You can perform the following operation at most 20√óN20 \times N20√óN times:


Select two indices i,ji, ji,j satisfying Ai‚Č†AjA_i \neq A_jAi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Aj‚Äč, and set both AiA_iAi‚Äč and AjA_jAj‚Äč to Ai‚äēAjA_i \oplus A_jAi‚Äč‚äēAj‚Äč.

Here, ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
Your goal is to make the final array sorted in non-decreasing order. Find a sequence of operations that achieves this.

It can be proven that a solution always exists.

Note that you don't have to minimize the number of operations.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the size of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format

For each test case, first print a single integer KKK, the number of operations you performed.
Then, print KKK lines each containing two integers. The integers on the iii-th line should be the indices you selected for the iii-th operation.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

The array becomes [60,40,20,60][60, 40, 20, 60][60,40,20,60] after the first operation. (Setting A1A_1A1‚Äč and A4A_4A4‚Äč to 34‚äē3034 \oplus 3034‚äē30)
The array becomes [40,40,40,60][40, 40, 40, 60][40,40,40,60] after the second operation. (Setting A1A_1A1‚Äč and A3A_3A3‚Äč to 60‚äē2060 \oplus 2060‚äē20)

Test case 222:

The array is already sorted, so we don't need to perform any operations.
